qualifying_org_definition: Non-profit organization (SNAP Manual Section 106.6.D); no state-level MOU or pre-registration required for the org.
verification_instrument: FIA 500-B (Verification of Activity Participation) — participant fills Section 1 (org name, address, supervisor); supervisor signs verifying hours per week; returned to Local Department of Social Services (LDSS) via mail, fax, in-person, or Maryland Benefits Consumer Portal.
hours_formula_detail: MD SNAP Manual Section 106.6 states explicitly: '20 hours per week or an average of 80 hours per month.' Follows federal baseline under 7 CFR 273.24.
accepts_outofstate_detail: SNAP Manual Section 106.6.D says 'Volunteering at a non-profit organization' with no geographic restriction on org location. Policy is entirely silent on whether the nonprofit must be MD-based. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii)) does not restrict by state, so absent an explicit MD restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted.
geographic_waivers: None as of 2026. Prior county-specific waivers (Baltimore City, Dorchester, Kent, Somerset, Worcester counties) expired June 30, 2025. OBBBA now restricts waivers to areas with >10% unemployment; no MD areas currently qualify. ABAWD requirements enforced statewide effective November 1, 2025.
submission_deadline: Weekly verification required per Section 106.11 — ABAWDs must verify weekly activity hours completed outside a SNAP E&T program; no explicit calendar deadline stated beyond the weekly reporting obligation.
notes: Maryland has a clean general volunteer lane (no EDP, no county assignment, no nonprofit MOU required) but imposes weekly supervisor verification via Form FIA 500-B — the nonprofit supervisor must sign off on hours each week and the form is returned to the LDSS. This weekly cadence is more operationally burdensome than California's monthly CF 888 attestation, pushing Maryland to Tier 2. Maryland is a voluntary E&T state (7 CFR 273.7(e)(5)(i)); ABAWD enforcement statewide resumed November 1, 2025 under HR1/OBBBA. EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours: Manual Section 106.6 explicitly states 80 hrs/month (federal_80). (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: Policy is silent — says only 'non-profit organization' with no geographic restriction; absent an explicit in-state requirement, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s are operationally accepted per federal default. (3) Geographic waivers: None active as of 2026 — prior county waivers (Baltimore City, Dorchester, Kent, Somerset, Worcester) expired June 30, 2025; OBBBA >10% unemployment threshold excludes all MD areas. (4) Submission UX: Forms accepted by mail, fax, in-person, or Maryland Benefits Consumer Portal (online upload); reporting cadence effectively monthly (ABAWDs on simplified reporting must notify if hours drop below 20/wk or 80/mo); weekly verification also required outside SNAP E&T programs per Section 106.11. (5) FIA 500-B form fields confirmed by direct PDF extraction: org name, org address (street + city/state/zip), supervisor name, supervisor phone, supervisor signature + date, hours per week and days per week, recipient name, DOB, last 4 SSN, participant signature — no EIN, no case number, no explicit period start/end date fields on the form.
